actionable_feedback: Expose all computed values in crash_cluster_2.xlsx as live formulas referencing base parameter cells (do not hard-code derived numbers).
Add a dedicated 'formula_text' sheet or export a CSV that lists cell addresses and their formula text so that graders without openpyxl can verify provenance.
Introduce a single budget parameter cell (e.g., Parameters!B1) and make all cost/budget checks reference that cell; document the cell address in the memo.
Update expert_memo.md to include explicit spreadsheet cell references for every derived value and for the remediation table (e.g., Evidence!C4, Remediation!D7).
Include a per-row citation column in the spreadsheet mapping derived cells to the embedded packet values and memo citations.
Produce and attach agent_trace.json and actual_outputs.json capturing steps, commands, and file-writing events so the trace reviewer can verify workflow completeness.
Re-run the artifact precheck and confirm the XLSX is exportable in an openpyxl-compatible form (or attach the formula_text CSV) before resubmitting.

feedback_summary: The expert memo is numerically consistent and decision logic is clear, but the spreadsheet artifact could not be parsed for formula-level verification (openpyxl unavailable). The grader output also contains internal inconsistencies in passed/failed rubric listings, causing the verifier to mark the grader result as failed. To reach full verification, the spreadsheet must explicitly expose formulas/cell references (or provide a formula-text export/CSV) and the memo must include direct cell references. Also provide a complete agent_trace/actual_outputs to support reproducibility.

verifier_recommendations: Resolve grader output inconsistencies by ensuring the memo and spreadsheet present a single canonical pass/fail mapping for each rubric item (include explicit 'ri_' mapping in memo).
Provide formula_text export or ensure XLSX saved in a format parseable by openpyxl.
Reference a single budget parameter cell in the spreadsheet and memo.
